knownIssues: RES4LYF registers extra samplers (res_2s, res_2m, ...) and schedulers (bong_tangent, ...) into the core KSampler dropdowns. If the package is not installed on the target, those enum values are silently absent and ComfyUI rejects the prompt with `'res_2s' not in (list)` — a core node (KSampler, cnr_id comfy-core) whose dropdown was extended by this package, so node-type scanning misses the dependency entirely.

The apple-to-apple fix is to install this package on the target so the values exist natively. Substituting to the closest core value (res_2s -> res_multistep, bong_tangent -> normal) changes the denoising trajectory and breaks pixel-level fidelity; it is a human-approved last resort only.

workarounds.action: Install RES4LYF on the target: git clone https://github.com/ClownsharkBatwing/RES4LYF into ComfyUI/custom_nodes, install its requirements, restart ComfyUI, then re-fetch /object_info and confirm the enum value (e.g. res_2s) now appears under KSampler.sampler_name.
workarounds.rationale: Restores the exact sampler/scheduler the source workflow used — keeps output identical (apple-to-apple).
workarounds.tradeoff: Adds one custom-node dependency to deploy on each target node. Prefer installing to a shared node library so it accumulates across nodes.

workarounds.action: LAST RESORT (requires explicit human approval, flag as fidelity-degrading): substitute to the closest core value — sampler_name res_2s -> res_multistep, scheduler bong_tangent -> normal — via runtime-policy JSON.
workarounds.rationale: Lets the workflow run when the package genuinely cannot be installed (repo unreachable / XPU-incompatible).
workarounds.tradeoff: Different denoising algorithm + sigma schedule -> visibly different image. NOT apple-to-apple. Must be human-approved and recorded.

retireCondition: ComfyUI core adds res_2s / bong_tangent to its built-in sampler/scheduler lists, making the package unnecessary.
